Red Velvet (레드벨벳) 是韓國SM Entertainment時隔五年後，於2014年推出的女子組合，由成員Irene、Seul Gi、Wendy、Joy以及2015年加入的Yeri 五人組成。
團名「Red Velvet」是將充滿強烈、魅惑的色彩「紅色 (Red)」與能夠同時讓人感受到柔軟、女性化的「天鵝絨 (Velvet)」結合在一起的名字，期望將來 Red Velvet 不管在音樂上或者表演上，能夠同時展現強悍與柔軟的多樣面貌，帶給大家不同面向的感官享受。

啤啤花名之解釋
Irene : 高祖奶奶，古代人，簡稱高祖
由來係因為HOT JOY 喺UFO一句回覆…
“我地個高祖奶奶唔識上網”
seulgi : 宅熊
因為無野做就會留係屋企睇劇 FF 自己係女主角…
再加上自稱小熊
wendy : 表情帝，承歡媽媽，我女人(啤 post 專用花名)
每次出show 做節目都可以有無限個表情…
平時係宿舍負責煮野食照顧其他成員，所以有媽媽之稱。
因某癡線佬經常以 "我女人" 專稱承歡，久而久之啤啤 post 各癡線佬習慣咗以 "我女人" 為承歡花名。
JOY: 肥JOY, 騙徒JOY, 門面JOY
減肥前︰肥JOY 不解釋…
減肥後︰門面實力驚為天人，門面JOY 歸位
HOT JOY︰ 已經靚到唔可以用門面黎形容....
騙徒源於肥JOY 無懈可擊既自拍技術
因為自拍出色，曾經俾人誤會係門面擔當
肥JOY自拍同飯拍完全係兩個人黎…
YERI: 車厘，西面車厘
車厘係因為佢自稱似車厘龜
西面係因為朝早唔夠訓又要出席活動既時候就會經常西面，啤啤 post 所有巴打最愛

跟住拜間媽 feel到氣氛開始高漲 仲要接多首神曲RR 開始聲沙 之後仲接Power Up主打3連 真係high爆

之後終於都開始第一次開場白.. 都過咗成粒鐘 開始9up話頭先發夢 啱啱唔係佢地嚟..

去到Umpah Umpah 第一次聽live嘅歌 副歌嗌Umpah Umpah喊到口乾 好正 之後竟然有我TRS隻碟入面第二中意嗰隻歌mojito 完全估唔到 不過佢地掛住掉波冇乜點唱 可能睇唔到歌詞係咁.. 有巴打報料Hot Joy直接遞個波俾個鬼佬.. 去到Carpool 係Hot Joy ig story劇透咗會有嘅歌 睇住佢地玩得好開心 變咗全場大合唱 臨尾個副歌到有個一字排開tun車嘅動作 好qq

最後一條片 又係睇佢地玩得好開心 之後就有愛歌Butterflies (車厘媽唔中意) 呢首編舞真係好靚 都係第一次聽live 跟住就應該係冇人唔中意嘅You Better Know 全場企起晒 氣氛應該去到最高點 第三首聽得返嘅Red Room歌 之後接Hit that drum 都估唔到會有呢首 應該都係因為要再炒場 啲人的確越嚟越high.. 最後Zimzalabim 真係真高潮 副歌嗰度盡顯控場威力 全場紅晒 一齊唸咒 真係成個邪教咁..

之後嗌encore又係唱歌時間 兩日都有唱 學咗少少韓文有啲信心 但成日有人望過嚟.. 之後就嗌레드 同 벨벳 兩日都嗌벨벳 真係冇聲咁滯..

終於出返嚟啦 又係唱估唔到嘅歌 Time to Love 呢首的確好聽 之後佢叫我地再唱多次嗰陣 第二日企S4 去到嗰陣已經推到好前 唔知係咪我錯覺 竟然見到歡同我對望睇住我唱.. 唔肯定佢係咪focus緊我 不過我當係架啦 諗返都好痴線.. 真最後一首 竟然唔係好正嘅Ladies Night.. 首尾呼應Somethin' Kinda Crazy.. 唔知好笑定好喊 不過算啦 未聽過live 之後就真係完啦...


整體應該三次con入面最高汁 成個編排好正 con concept真係120分 可能因為上年Red Mare真係差啲 得動物同鬼屋part最正 但今次係全場都正到爆 第一節開頭已經狂唱 中間啲過場BGM真係好有feel 係咁喺度諗講野未呀 連唱咁多首唔攰架咩？？？ 台中間個圓形升降台都好好 用咗幾次 上到去仲識轉 幾過癮 我企S4有啲位佢地行埋嚟都幾近 但最近都係上年玩車仔 歡喺正我前面停低 喊死
副台又上咗幾次 基本上好似每個區都有幾次近睇嘅機會 都okay 個場坐得多人 但睇落又唔算大 我覺得係細場先可以坐位都睇得清 但個場音響同個mon又虧虧地 cut shot嗰個又cut到9唔搭8 個場地理位置又棘 下年都係第二個場啦.. 今次咁正 有台場嘅話一定再去 澳門場嘅話就平啲 港場就瞓啦

Billboard The 100 Greatest K-Pop Songs of the 2010s: Staff List

https://www.billboard.com/articles/news/list/8544710/best-k-pop-songs-2010s-top-100

27. Red Velvet, “Bad Boy” (The Perfect Red Velvet, 2018)

While Red Velvet had played with the idea of something darker in the occultish extravaganza of “Peek-a-Boo,” their complete surrender to the darkness in “Bad Boy” had us reaching for Holy Water. Counted as their most daring release, the sultry, sensual, and tantalizing “Bad Boy” is the perfect representation of their mature “velvet” persona. Most remarkable, however, is how tactile the sonic and visual layering of the song is -- touching on the softness of each member before swelling into a sweeping chorus of harmonies. It speaks to the underlying emotion of donning your armor for a boy who’s obviously bad news. The Perfect Red Velvet, indeed. -- L.S.

2. Red Velvet, “Red Flavor” (The Red Summer, 2017)

The best pop songs can take a basic human emotion and make it the biggest, most extraordinary thing in the world -- despite such familiarity, certain feelings can be rendered anew through precise distillation and amplification. It’s the reason “Red Flavor” was such an undeniable hit since day one and remains ever-beloved by fans worldwide -- it overflows with an exuberance that’s palpable from the very first second.

Red Velvet have one of the most impressive and wide-ranging discographies of any K-pop group ever. From the sophisticated R&B throwback “Automatic” to the frenetic Shibuya-kei-indebted “Russian Roulette,” the ornate balladry of “One of These Nights” to the brass-and-rap hypnotism of “Dumb Dumb,” they could never be pinned down by their self-imposed “Red” and “Velvet” concepts. “Red Flavor” is most akin to the dubstep cheerleader romp of “Ice Cream Cake” -- itself a symbolic baton pass from f(x) to Red Velvet as the best idol group alive -- but packages its kitchen-sink extravagance into an easily digestible form.

Most importantly, the song’s evocations of a bright summer romance are felt in every flourish and quirk. There’s swirling chimes and girlish sighs, chipper woodblocks and resounding horn stabs, drumline percussion and flagellating vocal samples -- production and songwriting duo Caesar & Loui brought everything to the table, because a lover can change everything about your outlook on life. Beyond the bricolage, “Red Flavor” features moments of sweet repose that lead to a final, heartfelt confession that the “summer flavor” they adore so much is “you.” In three short minutes, Red Velvet make tangible the most ineffable of things: love. -- J.M.K.

I saw Red Velvet at Tokyo Dome Stadium twice which were marvelous and unforgettable. The first time I saw their concert with my friends we were late. I remember I said "Look at the time. The concert will have begun" It was ten to nine. The concert would have started by then. It was a worthwhile concert that was compelling enough to see again. They've been strutting their stuff since they were children so don't sell them short. At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause. They met with a thunderous ovation. The group and the dancers and acrobats who were invited to perform alongside the group gave a superb performance. The choreography, the stage and lighting design and the glow sticks were magnificent and beyond our expectations. They're very career-oriented. My uncle who too is very career-minded has been wondering about having them endorse his company's products but he'll have to make it financially worthwhile for them. They have a worthwhile job and career. I consider music performing a worthwhile career.

Billboard The 25 Greatest K-Pop Albums of the 2010s: Staff List

5. Red Velvet, Perfect Velvet (2017)

A truly consistent pop album is hard to come by, but Red Velvet have made a few of them to date -- and Perfect Velvet may be their crowning jewel. The sinister tropical house of lead single “Peek-a-Boo” sets the stage for a literal horror show, with the song’s video featuring all five members tormenting an unsuspecting pizza guy in a Hollywood bungalow. The whole album is punctuated by this candy-coated violence -- a core theme of Red Velvet’s discography -- and blown to dizzying proportions, even on tracks that don’t betray it at first glance.

“My Second Date” presents the outline of a midtempo R&B track, but is perforated by the paranoia of a second rendezvous -- from the toybox glockenspiel that traces out its melody, to the hyperactive dubstep of its chorus, to the manic guitar noodling that drops at around the halfway point. “I Just,” meanwhile, chugs along with locomotive urgency, a relentless meditation on turning the page in life. Red Velvet has always found euphoria amid the underlying menace of youth, and that anxiety shapes Perfect Velvet: a monumental work of discomfort and bliss. -- J.C.
